title: Extraction and PCR for Animal Samples Using TaqPath COVID-19 Combo Kit
author: Brianna Stenger
materials: Thermofisher Scientific TaqPath COVID-19 Combo Kit
Film or foil for sealing plates
If frozen, thaw the reagents on ice. Gently vortex the reagents, then centrifuge briefly to collect liquid at the bottom of the tubes.

steps.title: Prepare working areas by cleaning with...
steps.instruction: Prepare working areas by cleaning with ~10% bleach or ~70% alcohol, followed by a rinse and use of another solution to reduce the presence of RNases such as RNaseZAP or Eliminase.

steps.title: Start the run and load prepared...
steps.instruction: Start the run and load prepared plates in positions when prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: Immediately remove the elution plate from...
steps.instruction: Immediately remove the elution plate from the instrument and cover with film/foil. Run lasts ~24 minutes. Seal immediately to avoid evaporation and store up to 48hrs at 4°C, -20°C, or -70°C.

steps.title: Prepare Plates for extraction following table 2. Table 2
steps.instruction: Prepare Plates for extraction following table 2. Table 2: Plate setup. %s %s Plate Position Plate ID Plate Type Reagent Volume per Well 1 Sample Plate KingFisher™ Deepwell 96 Plate or Equivalent 10 µL Proteinase K + 400 µLSample + 550 µL Bead mix (new tips!) + 10 µL MS2 Phage Control 2 Wash 1 Wash Buffer 1000 µL 3 Wash 2 80% Ethanol 1000 µL 4 Elution Plate Elution Solution 50 µL 5 Tip Comb Place a KingFisher™ 96 tip comb for DW magnets in a KingFisher™ 96 KF microplate Handle samples according to institutional biosafety standards According to manufacterer, avoid DEPC water. It is recommend to cover filled wash and elution plates with a seal to avoid evaporation if it takes a while to load the sample plate. A new tip is recommended for each well when pipetting Bead/lysis mix due to foaming.

steps.title: Select the program MVP_2Wash_400_Flex on the...
steps.instruction: Select the program MVP_2Wash_400_Flex on the KingFisher™ Flex Magnetic Particle Processor with 96 Deep-Well Head

steps.title: Mix well by inversion, then store at room temperature
steps.instruction: Mix well by inversion, then store at room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare Binding Beads/Lysis mix on each day of use
steps.instruction: Prepare Binding Beads/Lysis mix on each day of use.

steps.title: Prepare a Master Mix of the...
steps.instruction: Prepare a Master Mix of the PCR reagents containing enough for all samples, controls, plus extra. Master Mix is prepared manually using the volumes in Table 3. Table 3. PCR Reagent Volumes. %s %s Nuclease-free Water 12.5 4X TaqPath 1-Step Multiplex MMX (No ROX) 6.25 COVID-19 Real Time PCR Assay Multiplex 1.25 µl MM per rxn 20 Template DNA 5 Total Volume 25

steps.title: Add 5 µL of extraction RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of extraction RNA or negative control to corresponding well. Total reaction volume will be 25 µL Sample plate can be vortexed and centrifuged briefly to collect liquid to the bottom of the plate.

steps.title: Add 2 µL of diluted positive...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of diluted positive control + 3 µL of nuclease-free water for positive control well.

steps.title: Diluted positive control is made by
steps.instruction: Diluted positive control is made by: Pipet 98 µL of TaqPath™ COVID‑19 Control Dilution Buffer into a microcentrifuge tube, then add 2 µL of TaqPath™ COVID‑19 Control. Mix well, then centrifuge briefly

steps.title: Pipet 87
steps.instruction: Pipet 87.5 µL of TaqPath™ COVID‑19 Control Dilution Buffer into a second microcentrifuge tube, then add 12.5 µL of the dilution created in substep 13.1. Mix well, then centrifuge briefly. The positive control does not contain the MS2 phage.

steps.title: Once sample and controls have been added, seal plate thoroughly
steps.instruction: Once sample and controls have been added, seal plate thoroughly.

steps.title: Pipette 20 µL of master mix into each well
steps.instruction: Pipette 20 µL of master mix into each well.

steps.title: Vortex plate at high speed for 10-30 seconds
steps.instruction: Vortex plate at high speed for 10-30 seconds. Recommend a plate holder attachment to avoid damaging wells.

steps.title: Centrifuge the reaction plate for 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the reaction plate for 2 minutes (~600 x g) to remove bubbles and collect liquid to the bottom of the reaction plate. Speed may vary based on plate spinner. Manufacturer recommended ≥ 650 x g.

steps.title: For the number of required reactions,...
steps.instruction: For the number of required reactions, prepare the Binding Bead/Lysis Mix according to Table 1 Table 1: Binding Bead/Lysis Mix %s %s Component Volume per RNA Sample or Control Volume for 110 rxns Binding Solution 530 μL 58.3 mL Nucleic Acid Magnetic Beads 20 μL 2.2 mL Total Reaction Mix volume 550 μL 60.5 mL Human protocol does not allow for combining PK, phage, beads and binding solution all together; however, it worked for animal samples. Saves a significant amount of time and tips.

steps.title: Vortex the Total Nucleic Acid Magnetic...
steps.instruction: Vortex the Total Nucleic Acid Magnetic Beads to ensure that the bead mixture is homogeneous.

steps.title: Invert the Binding bead Mix (from 2
steps.instruction: Invert the Binding bead Mix (from 2.2) five times gently to mix, then add 570 µL (binding solution + beads + PK + MS2 phage control) to each sample well and negative control well in the sample plate. Remix the Binding Bead Mix by inversion frequently during pipetting to ensure even distribution of beads to all samples or wells. The Binding Bead Mix is viscous, so pipet slowly to ensure that the correct amount is added. DO NOT reuse pipette tips to add Binding Bead Mix to the samples, as the high viscosity will cause variations in the volumes added.

steps.title: Add samples and controls to the appropriate wells
steps.instruction: Add samples and controls to the appropriate wells: Samples: 400 µL of sample to a well. Negative Control: 400 µL L of Nuclease-free Water (not DEPC-Treated) to the Negative Control If use a 96 well plate: up to 94 samples can be on ran, two wells are reserved for controls, one for the negative extraction control and one for the positive amplification control that is not added until the PCR step (step 6).

steps.title: Seal sample plate with film
steps.instruction: Seal sample plate with film. Wipe outside of plate with disinfectant and place in transport container. Wipe/disinfect transport container and carefully bring to BSC in Extraction Room.

steps.title: Setting up Thermal Cycler This protocol...
steps.instruction: Setting up Thermal Cycler This protocol used the ABI 7500 Fast or ABI 7500 DX thermal cycler. Set up thermal cycler according to manufacture instruction manuals along with table 4 and 5. Refer to https://www.fda.gov/media/136112/download in reference sections for more information

steps.title: Table 4
steps.instruction: Table 4. Target, dyes, and quencher. A B C 1 Target Reporter dye Quencher 2 ORF1ab FAM None 3 N gene VIC None 4 S gene ABY None 5 MS2 JUN None

steps.title: Table 5
steps.instruction: Table 5. Thermal cycler conditions. A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Number of cycles 2 UNG incubation 25 C 2 min 1 3 Reverse transcription 53 C 10 min 1 4 Activation 95 C 2 min 1 5 Denaturation 95 C 3 sec 40 6 Anneal/extension 60 C 30 sec

steps.title: A minimum of one Negative Control...
steps.instruction: A minimum of one Negative Control and one Positive Control must be present for each run. Additional Negative Control wells must be run for each extraction that is represented on a real-time RT-PCR plate. All control wells must pass for the real-time RT-PCR plate to be considered valid.

steps.title: Validation of results is performed automatically...
steps.instruction: Validation of results is performed automatically by the Applied Biosystems™ COVID‑19 Interpretive Software based on performance of the Positive, Negative, and Internal Controls. See Table 6. Table 6. Interpretation of Results Inconclusive sample should be re-extracted and retested one time.
